text: Consider a React component that fetches data on mount and updates state. Which of the following are best practices for managing side effects and preventing memory leaks?

options.A: Using a cleanup function in `useEffect` to cancel subscriptions or set flags.
options.B: Ensuring all state updates are wrapped in `setTimeout` to defer execution.
options.C: Adding `isMounted` flag to prevent state updates on unmounted components.
options.D: Placing the `fetch` call directly inside the component body without `useEffect`.

text: In an Express.js application, what is the correct order of middleware execution for a request that matches `/api/users`?

options.B: Global 1 -> Resource specific -> /api/users
options.C: Global 1 -> /api/users
options.D: Global 1 -> Global 2 -> Resource specific -> /api/users

text: You have a MongoDB collection `orders` with documents like `{ _id: 1, userId: 'A', total: 100, items: [{productId: 'P1', qty: 2}, {productId: 'P2', qty: 1}] }`. Which aggregation pipeline stages can you use to find the total quantity of 'P1' ordered by each user?

options.B: `$match` to filter for `productId: 'P1'`.
options.C: `$group` by `userId` and sum `items.qty`.
options.D: `$lookup` to join with a `products` collection.

text: Which Tailwind CSS class would you use to make an element's text color blue, with a shade of 500, and make the text bold?

options.B: `color-blue-500 font-weight-bold`
options.C: `text-blue-500 text-bold`
options.D: `blue-500 bold`

text: In React, you have a parent component passing a callback function and an object prop to a child component. The child component frequently re-renders. How can you optimize the child component to prevent unnecessary re-renders?

options.B: Use `useCallback` for the `onClick` prop in the `Parent` component.
options.C: Use `useMemo` for the `data` prop in the `Parent` component.
options.D: Convert `Child` to a class component and implement `shouldComponentUpdate`.

text: You are building a REST API with Express.js. Which of the following are good practices for handling errors consistently across your application?

options.A: Defining a centralized error-handling middleware at the end of your middleware stack.
options.B: Using `try...catch` blocks in every asynchronous route handler to catch errors.
options.C: Wrapping async route handlers with a utility function that catches errors and passes them to `next()`.
options.D: Returning a 200 OK status with an error message in the response body for all errors.

text: Which type of MongoDB index would be most beneficial for queries that frequently sort documents by a specific field in ascending order?

options.B: Hashed Index
options.C: Single Field Index (ascending)
options.D: Geospatial Index

text: In React, when does the cleanup function returned by `useEffect` typically execute?

options.B: Before the component renders for the first time.
options.C: Before the effect runs again due to a dependency change, and when the component unmounts.
options.D: After every render, regardless of dependencies.

text: You want to design a responsive layout using Tailwind CSS where a `div` takes full width on small screens, half width on medium screens, and one-third width on large screens. Which of the following class combinations would achieve this?

options.B: `w-full sm:w-1/2 md:w-1/3`
options.C: `col-span-12 md:col-span-6 lg:col-span-4` (within a grid)
options.D: `width-full medium:width-half large:width-third`

text: When implementing user authentication in an Express.js application using JSON Web Tokens (JWTs), which of the following are crucial considerations?
code:
const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
const token = jwt.sign({ userId: user._id }, process.env.JWT_SECRET, { expiresIn: '1h' });

options.A: Storing the JWT in `localStorage` on the client-side for persistent sessions.
options.B: Using HTTPS to protect JWTs during transmission.
options.C: Implementing middleware to verify the JWT on protected routes.
options.D: Storing sensitive user data directly within the JWT payload.

text: You have a deeply nested component tree in React, and several components need access to the current user's authentication status. What is the most idiomatic way to share this state without prop drilling?

options.A: Pass the authentication status as props through every component in the tree.
options.B: Use the React Context API to provide the authentication status.
options.C: Store the authentication status in a global JavaScript variable.
options.D: Use a state management library like Redux or Zustand.

text: You are designing a MongoDB schema for a blog application. Each `post` can have multiple `comments`. What are valid strategies for storing comments related to a post?

options.A: Embedding comments directly within the `post` document if comments are few and rarely updated.
options.B: Creating a separate `comments` collection and referencing `postId` in each comment document.
options.C: Using a `DBRef` to link `post` and `comment` documents, which is MongoDB's recommended approach for all relationships.
options.D: Storing only the count of comments in the `post` document and fetching comments separately.

text: Which of the following Node.js features is commonly used in Express.js route handlers to manage asynchronous operations like database queries or external API calls?

options.B: Promises with `async/await`
options.C: Event Emitters
options.D: Child Processes

text: How can you add a custom color, say `brand-purple`, to your Tailwind CSS configuration so you can use classes like `text-brand-purple`?

options.B: Add `brand-purple: '#6B46C1'` to `theme.extend.colors`.
options.C: Define it as a CSS variable and reference it in `tailwind.config.js`.
options.D: It's not possible to add custom colors; you must use existing ones.

text: You need to implement a reusable piece of logic in React that manages a counter with increment and decrement functions, and also persists the counter value to `localStorage`. What is the most appropriate React feature for this?

options.B: A Render Prop component.
options.C: A custom Hook.
options.D: A utility function imported into each component.

text: In MongoDB, when would you typically use a multi-document transaction?

options.B: When updating multiple documents in a single collection that are not related.
options.C: When you need to ensure atomicity and consistency across multiple write operations on different documents or collections.
options.D: When querying data across multiple collections using `$lookup`.

text: When designing a RESTful API with Express.js for managing a resource like `products`, which HTTP methods and corresponding actions are generally considered best practice?

options.A: GET `/products` to retrieve a list of products.
options.B: POST `/products` to create a new product.
options.C: PUT `/products/:id` to partially update an existing product.
options.D: DELETE `/products/:id` to remove a specific product.

text: You are building a form in React with multiple input fields. Which of the following are recommended approaches for managing form state and handling input changes efficiently?

options.A: Using a single state object to hold all form field values.
options.B: Creating a separate `useState` hook for each individual input field.
options.C: Implementing a generic `handleChange` function that updates state based on input `name` attribute.
options.D: Allowing input fields to manage their own state (uncontrolled components) for complex forms.

text: Which of the following statements about Tailwind CSS and its features (like JIT mode or plugins) are true?

options.A: JIT (Just-In-Time) mode compiles CSS on demand as you write HTML, leading to faster development and smaller file sizes.
options.B: Tailwind plugins allow you to add new utility classes or extend existing ones.
options.C: Tailwind CSS requires you to write custom CSS for every single style property.
options.D: Tailwind's utility-first approach means you never need to write any custom CSS.

text: Your React frontend application, running on `http://localhost:3000`, needs to make API requests to your Express.js backend, running on `http://localhost:5000`. What common issue might you encounter, and how is it typically resolved in Express.js?

options.A: Authentication errors, resolved by sending JWTs in headers.
options.B: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) errors, resolved by using the `cors` middleware.
options.C: Routing conflicts, resolved by ensuring unique API endpoints.
options.D: Database connection issues, resolved by configuring MongoDB URI.

text: In a React application using React Router DOM, how would you programmatically navigate a user to a different route after a successful form submission?

options.B: By importing and calling `useNavigate()` hook and then `navigate('/dashboard')`.
options.C: By rendering a `<Redirect to="/dashboard" />` component.
options.D: By directly manipulating the browser history object.

text: For a high-traffic MongoDB application requiring high availability and horizontal scalability, which features are essential to consider?

options.A: Replication (Replica Sets) for data redundancy and automatic failover.
options.B: Sharding for distributing data across multiple servers and improving write/read capacity.
options.C: Using `db.collection.find().limit(1)` for all queries to reduce load.
options.D: Implementing a robust indexing strategy to optimize query performance.

text: You are building a real-time chat application using Node.js. Which technologies or concepts are most suitable for enabling instant, bidirectional communication between the server and clients?

options.A: Long Polling with standard HTTP requests.
options.B: WebSockets (e.g., using Socket.IO).
options.C: Server-Sent Events (SSE).
options.D: Regular REST API calls with short intervals.

text: In a React application, a component deep in the tree throws an unhandled JavaScript error during rendering. Which of the following mechanisms can prevent this error from crashing the entire application and display a fallback UI?

options.A: Using `try...catch` blocks around the problematic component's JSX.
options.B: Implementing an Error Boundary component.
options.C: Wrapping the root component in a global `window.onerror` handler.
options.D: Using `useEffect` with a cleanup function to catch errors.

text: You need to store a user's date of birth in a MongoDB document. Which BSON data type is generally recommended for storing dates and times?

options.B: Date
options.C: Timestamp
options.D: Number (Unix epoch)
